mechanism_name: Lever Arm Length and Effort Savings
commonsense_rule: Lever arm length ↑ (fulcrum closer to the load) → fewer weights are needed to lift the load (less effort)
factual_rule: Lever arm length ↑ (fulcrum closer to the load) → fewer weights are needed to lift the load (less effort)
flipped_rule: Lever arm length ↑ (fulcrum closer to the load) → more weights are needed to lift the load (more effort)

question: Complete the rule suggested by the image: when the effort arm becomes shorter, the number of standard weights needed to balance the lever will ____.

choices.A: decrease in this world.
choices.B: stay the same.
choices.C: determine how long the effort arm becomes.
choices.D: increase.

prior_trap.reason: It matches ordinary lever intuition from everyday physics.

partial_error.reason: It ignores that the image links changes in arm length to changes in the amount of balancing weight.

causal_confusion.reason: It reverses the direction of causation between lever geometry and required effort.

reasoning_chain: In the image, longer effort arms are paired with larger numbers of green standard weights while the lever stays balanced. Completing the opposite side of that rule means that making the effort arm shorter must reduce the number of standard weights needed in this world.
required_induction: In this world, increasing the effort arm increases the required balancing weight, so decreasing the effort arm lowers it.

commonsense_rule: Lever arm length ↑ (fulcrum closer to the load) → fewer weights are needed to lift the load (less effort)
factual_rule: Lever arm length ↑ (fulcrum closer to the load) → fewer weights are needed to lift the load (less effort)
flipped_rule: Lever arm length ↑ (fulcrum closer to the load) → more weights are needed to lift the load (more effort)

mechanism_name: Rotation Speed and Centrifugal Deflection Angle
commonsense_rule: Rotation speed ↑ → the angle between the hanging object and the vertical direction ↑ (closer to horizontal)
